mnemonics: Deep Learning is like a neural net in your brain: It learns patterns layer by layer (DEEP) to make sense of complex information, just like you're doing now!
keyPoints: Deep Learning uses multi-layered neural networks to learn hierarchical representations of data
It excels at identifying complex patterns in unstructured data like images, sound, and text
The 'deep' refers to many hidden layers between input and output layers
commonMistakes: Thinking deep learning requires human-like consciousness (it's pattern recognition, not general intelligence)
Assuming more layers always improve performance (can lead to overfitting)
Confusing deep learning with all machine learning (it's a subset)
